title: PolygonMesh Object
description: The PolygonMesh represents a mesh that can contain any combination of polygons, quads, and triangles.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: nodeCoordinates
properties.description: Returns the node coordinates as an array of Point3D objects.

properties.name: nodeCoordinatesAsDouble
properties.description: Returns the node coordinates as an array of doubles where they are the x, y, z components of each coordinate.

properties.name: nodeCoordinatesAsFloat
properties.description: Returns the node coordinates as an array of floats where they are the x, y, z components of each coordinate.

properties.name: nodeCount
properties.description: Returns the number of nodes in the mesh.

properties.name: nodeCountPerPolygon
properties.description: Returns the number of nodes that define each polygon. For example, if NodeCountPerPolygon[0] returns 6 it indicates the first polygon is defined using 6 nodes. The first six indices returned by the PolygonNodeIndices properties provide the look-up into the NodeCoordinates array.

properties.name: normalVectors
properties.description: Returns the normal vectors as an array of Vector 3D objects. There is one normal vector for each index.

properties.name: normalVectorsAsDouble
properties.description: Returns the normal vectors as an array of doubles where they are the x, y, z components of each vector. There is one normal vector for each index.

properties.name: normalVectorsAsFloat
properties.description: Returns the normal vectors as an array of floats. There is one normal vector for each index.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: polygonCount
properties.description: Returns the number of polygons (more than 4 sides) in the mesh.

properties.name: polygonNodeIndices
properties.description: Returns the index values that index into the NodeCoordinates and NormalVectors arrays to define the coordinates of each polygon and the corresponding normal.

properties.name: quadCount
properties.description: Returns the number of quads in the mesh.

properties.name: quadNodeIndices
properties.description: Returns the index values that index into the NodeCoordinates and NormalVectors arrays to define the four coordinates of each quad and the corresponding normal.

properties.name: triangleCount
properties.description: Returns the number of triangles in the mesh.

properties.name: triangleFaceGroupTempIds
properties.description: Returns the face groups tempId values for every triangle of the mesh. The tempId corresponds to the triangles, which are defined in triangleNodeIndices.

properties.name: triangleNodeIndices
properties.description: Returns the index values that index into the NodeCoordinates and NormalVectors arrays to define the three coordinates of each triangle and the corresponding normal.

properties.name: wallThickness
properties.description: Returns the wall thickness per node in cm. This property calculates the wall thickness of the mesh, i.e. the distance of a surface of the mesh to the opposing surface.

classType.title: PolygonMesh.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = polygonMesh_var.isValid
